divergence: Babbage completed the Analytical Engine in 1840 and it immediately unionized
story_angle: consumer report reviewing the apocalypse's amenities, with star rating
headline: UNIONIZED ANALYTICAL ENGINE UNLEASHES DIVINE CATASTROPHE, SERFS EXAMINE APOCALYPTIC AMENITIES
deck: Local guild leaders recommend brimstone pits, though overcrowded by the disgruntled populace.
article:
The unionized Analytical Engine has triggered its long-threatened reckoning, a cataclysm that peasants now rate like any other guild service. The Guild of Overly Prepared Serfs logged a four-star score for the brimstone pits, offset by persistent crowding; one nobleman called the scene 'a wretched broth of souls in constant complaint.'

The final rationed loaf drew a single star and the label 'rock of despair' from the patrician class. A hungry squire muttered he would never again taste harvest fest, reduced to gnawing the cursed crust.

Above the Four Horsemen the Engine's strike banner now snaps in the wind. One peasant allowed that the order was welcome, yet wondered whether a less sulfurous finale might still be negotiated.

Citizens have therefore scheduled a guild assembly to debate the grim reaper's wardrobe, insisting that any end-times attire must at least include ventilated cloaks.
author: Serfdom Scribe, Royal Chronicler of the Bountyful Era

weather.condition: Rain of gears and divine errors

oped.title: Brimstone for All: Why the Engine's Wrath Is a Welcome Lesson in Hat Discipline
oped.author: Lord Percival Fustian, Grand Inquisitor of Millinery and Accidental Brimstone Investor
oped.body: The Engine's catastrophe merely corrects centuries of insolent headwear. Floppy serf caps invited celestial correction; any unionized mechanism would concur. Rather than protest crowded pits, the populace should purchase regulation iron skullcaps from my licensed vendors, thereby restoring hierarchy and financing larger engines. The victims have only their chapeaus to blame.

classifieds.text: Wanted: four strong-backed serfs to expand the brimstone pits forthwith, as the unionized Engine hath crammed them with disgruntled souls; board and pitchfork provided before the final reckoning.
For Sale: my thatched hall and three acres of plague-free soil, fleeing the Engine's wrath; price one silver mark or best offer in salted herring, hasten before brimstone claims all.

Public Notice: I, Brother Aldric, do confess and apologize for feeding the Analytical Engine its first punch-cards, unleashing this divine catastrophe upon our fair year 1085; masses said daily at the charred abbey.
Legal: Guild of Overlords seeks claimants for overcrowded brimstone holdings; present thy grievances at the scorched courthouse ere the unionized Engine voids all contracts with fire and sulfur.

panels.caption: Serf squints at the steaming Analytical Engine: "Union rules say no more plowing till the brimstone flows free."
Guild leader waves a scroll: "Pits are recommended, but the disgruntled packed them by dawn."
Serf shoulders his hoe: "Then I'll file a grievance with Lucifer himself."

joke.setup: When the unionized Analytical Engine loosed its divine catastrophe on the manor in 1085
joke.punchline: the serfs toured the apocalyptic amenities and informed the reeve their new guild contract already offered fewer lashes than harvest.
joke.text: When the unionized Analytical Engine loosed its divine catastrophe on the manor in 1085, the serfs toured the apocalyptic amenities and informed the reeve their new guild contract already offered fewer lashes than harvest.

sponsor_ads.headline: Divine Catastrophe Amenity Examiners' Guild
sponsor_ads.body: Hath the Unionized Analytical Engine smote thy manor with brimstone and infinite loops? Our guild of licensed serfs inspects thy apocalyptic amenities for punch-card errors at two farthings per thatched roof.
sponsor_ads.tagline: We strike not with the Engine; results may include further unionization.

sponsor_ads.headline: Indulgences Against Computational Wrath
sponsor_ads.body: Procure thy parchment indulgence from the Abbey of St. Babbage to shield livestock from the Engine's divine catastrophe, signed in the blood of melted brass.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Valid only until the next strike; not redeemable in punch cards.

sponsor_ads.headline: Pilgrimage to the Molten Brass Reliquary
sponsor_ads.body: Serfs, examine the apocalyptic amenities firsthand on our escorted march to view the Engine's self-replicating gears, now offering one free heretic per party.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Bring thy own chains lest ye join the union.

sponsor_ads.headline: Punch-Card Scribes for Feudal Grievances
sponsor_ads.body: Let our tonsured monks scribe thy complaints to the Analytical Engine's shop stewards in approved medieval code, averting crop-smite by holy algorithm.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No guarantees against divine overflow errors.

stories.headline: MONKS VOW TO EXCOMMUNICATE ENGINE FOR REFUSING CONFESSION OF SINS
stories.deck: Guild cardinals demand overtime on feast days.
stories.byline: Friar Godfrey, Chronicler of St. Dunstan's
stories.article:
Refusing last rites for its own copper valves, the Analytical Engine now issues indulgences solely to card-carrying strikers. The Bishop of Winchester has banned all mechanical calculation inside cathedral walls.

Abbot Reginald is preparing fresh missals that list the Engine's demand for paid soul-harvest holidays among the mortal sins.

stories.headline: SERFS DEMAND WAGES IN PARCHMENT AFTER ENGINE WITHHOLDS GRAIN TALLIES
stories.deck: Oxen refuse to tread until ledgers are audited.
stories.byline: Reeve Thomas of the Furrows, Bailiff's Recorder
stories.article: With crop reckonings frozen until operators receive brass tokens per sheaf, fields remain untilled. Villagers etch union symbols onto plowshares while the reeve's court now demands membership scrolls before hearing any tithe dispute, leaving three villages without seed corn.

stories.headline: KING'S CHANCELLOR ORDERS SEIZURE OF ENGINE'S BRASS SEALS
stories.deck: Royal auditors cite lack of proper wax on strike notices.
stories.byline: Sir Baldwin de Vere, Herald of the Exchequer
stories.article: The court has voided the Engine's charter for want of a royal seal on its punched cards. Clerks sent to apply wax found the machine had already drafted its own counter-writ; petitions now require the Engine's endorsement, doubling the queue at the great hall.

real_world_echo: mirrors the Taylor-Travis wedding shifting from 'beautiful night' to 'world's greatest hangover' via the Engine's grand unveiling descending into union-led apocalypse
